summary: See clues file for full details. 4 tossups, 6 bonuses found.

works.name: General / Key Works
works.description: Analysis from 4 tossups and 6 bonuses. See clues file for detailed breakdown.

clues.clue: While opening a chest with a blue and gold crown, a woman stares at this figure's "shadow of death" in one painting.
examples: While opening a chest with a blue and gold crown, a woman stares at this figure's "shadow of death" in one painting.

clues.clue: This figure is red-headed in a painting that Charles Dickens lambasted for its depiction of a woman painted "so horrible in her ugliness" as to appear in the "lowest gin-shop in England".
examples: This figure is red-headed in a painting that Charles Dickens lambasted for its depiction of a woman painted "so horrible in her ugliness" as to appear in the "lowest gin-shop in England".

clues.clue: A boy holding a water bowl stares at this figure as he's kissed by his mother, who is modeled on Mary Hodgkinson, in that painting.
examples: A boy holding a water bowl stares at this figure as he's kissed by his mother, who is modeled on Mary Hodgkinson, in that painting.

clues.clue: An artist worked on both a painting of this figure and The Lady of Shalott with Edward Robert Hughes.
examples: An artist worked on both a painting of this figure and The Lady of Shalott with Edward Robert Hughes.

clues.clue: In that painting, this figure holds a lantern in his left hand and knocks with his right; that William Holman Hunt painting is The Light of the World.
examples: In that painting, this figure holds a lantern in his left hand and knocks with his right; that William Holman Hunt painting is The Light of the World.

clues.clue: One painting this artist includes a blue wall design featuring dancing women bearing globes.
examples: One painting this artist includes a blue wall design featuring dancing women bearing globes.

clues.clue: A musical arrangement of Tennyson's Tears, Idle Tears lies discarded on the floor in one of this artist's works, while in another the central figure shows a girl a death's-head hawk moth.
examples: A musical arrangement of Tennyson's Tears, Idle Tears lies discarded on the floor in one of this artist's works, while in another the central figure shows a girl a death's-head hawk moth.

clues.clue: This artist who painted a version of the Lady of Shallott repainted his most famous work when he discovered that sixpence was being charged to see it by Keble College, Oxford.
examples: This artist who painted a version of the Lady of Shallott repainted his most famous work when he discovered that sixpence was being charged to see it by Keble College, Oxford.

clues.clue: This artist depicted Christ knocking on a door in The Light of the World.
examples: This artist depicted Christ knocking on a door in The Light of the World.

clues.clue: One character with this surname was described as an "immortal fuck" by Clay Regazzoni in a 2013 film.
examples: One character with this surname was described as an "immortal fuck" by Clay Regazzoni in a 2013 film.

clues.clue: A politician with this surname called Michael Gove "crass" for politicising the memory of the First World War, and this is also the surname of the painter who depicted a woman rising from her lover's lap in front of a piano in The Awakening Conscience.
examples: A politician with this surname called Michael Gove "crass" for politicising the memory of the First World War, and this is also the surname of the painter who depicted a woman rising from her lover's lap in front of a piano in The Awakening Conscience.

clues.clue: The job title of another man with this surname was notoriously spoonerised by James Naughtie during that man's tenure as Culture Secretary.
examples: The job title of another man with this surname was notoriously spoonerised by James Naughtie during that man's tenure as Culture Secretary.
